recommendation.explanation: This candidate activates multiple blood-relevant Sei classes including E5 B-cell-like (8.29, used as the on-target score), E7 Monocyte/Macrophage (6.34), and E11 T-cell (6.25), indicating broad hematopoietic regulatory logic consistent with a blood-targeting element. However, the top-scoring class is P Promoter (9.72) and E4 Multi-tissue (8.87) also scores very high, which together with a specificity ratio of only 0.85 suggests substantial non-blood activity — notably E6 Weak epithelial (7.31) could drive off-target expression in epithelial tissues during in vivo AAV delivery. While no synthesis or stability flags were raised (GC content is an acceptable 54.5%), the moderate specificity ratio and strong multi-tissue/promoter signatures warrant caution: this element may function effectively in hematopoietic cells but is unlikely to restrict expression exclusively to blood lineages, so additional regulatory tuning or miRNA-based detargeting strategies should be considered for applications requiring strict tissue specificity.

summary: This synthetic element strongly activates blood-related gene programs (B-cell, monocyte, T-cell) but also shows significant multi-tissue and epithelial activity, so it would likely drive robust expression in blood cells with some off-target expression in other tissues.
